questions.question: A beaker containing solid carbon dioxide is placed in a fume cupboard at room temperature. The carbon dioxide becomes gaseous. Which process describes this change of state? A boiling, B condensation, C evaporation, D sublimation

questions.question: The pressure of a sample of gas is decreased. The temperature is kept constant. Which row describes the effects on the particles? movement of particles collisions between particles A slower occur less often, B slower occur with more force, C no change in speed occur less often, D no change in speed occur with more force
questions.text: The pressure of a sample of gas is decreased. The temperature is kept constant. Which row describes the effects on the particles? movement of particles collisions between particles A slower occur less often, B slower occur with more force, C no change in speed occur less often, D no change in speed occur with more force
options: B slower occur with more force
C no change in speed occur less often
D no change in speed occur with more force

options: A A solvent is needed to dissolve the paper.
B Paper chromatography separates mixtures of solvents.
C The solvent should cover the baseline.
D The baseline should be drawn in pencil. 3

questions.question: Iodine,     I,  has  a  lower  relative  atomic  mass  than  tellurium,  Te,  but  is  placed  after  it  in  the Periodic Table. TeI Which statement explains why iodine is placed after tellurium in the Periodic Table? A Iodine has fewer neutrons than tellurium., B Iodine has fewer protons than tellurium., C Iodine has more neutrons than tellurium., D Iodine has more protons than tellurium. 4
questions.text: Iodine,     I,  has  a  lower  relative  atomic  mass  than  tellurium,  Te,  but  is  placed  after  it  in  the Periodic Table. TeI Which statement explains why iodine is placed after tellurium in the Periodic Table? A Iodine has fewer neutrons than tellurium., B Iodine has fewer protons than tellurium., C Iodine has more neutrons than tellurium., D Iodine has more protons than tellurium. 4
options: A Iodine has fewer neutrons than tellurium.
B Iodine has fewer protons than tellurium.
C Iodine has more neutrons than tellurium.
D Iodine has more protons than tellurium. 4

questions.question: Elements X and Y form an ionic compound, XY. In which group of the Periodic Table is X found and how is the bond between X and Y formed? group in which X is found how the bond between X and Y is formed A I by X gaining one electron from Y, B I by X transferring one electron to Y, C VII by X sharing electrons with Y, D VII by X transferring one electron to Y
questions.text: Elements X and Y form an ionic compound, XY. In which group of the Periodic Table is X found and how is the bond between X and Y formed? group in which X is found how the bond between X and Y is formed A I by X gaining one electron from Y, B I by X transferring one electron to Y, C VII by X sharing electrons with Y, D VII by X transferring one electron to Y
options: A I by X gaining one electron from Y
B I by X transferring one electron to Y
C VII by X sharing electrons with Y
D VII by X transferring one electron to Y

A Carbon monoxide is formed from the complete combustion of petroleum., B Lead compounds are formed from some types of petrol., C Oxides of nitrogen are formed from the combustion reactions inside car engines., D Sulfur dioxide is formed from the combustion of coal. 13

options: A Carbon monoxide is formed from the complete combustion of petroleum.
B Lead compounds are formed from some types of petrol.
C Oxides of nitrogen are formed from the combustion reactions inside car engines.
D Sulfur dioxide is formed from the combustion of coal. 13

questions.question: The table describes three types of water. water type source of water appearance before treatment treatment appearance after treatment P            river            muddy            none            muddy Q river muddy filtration and chlorination clear R            well            clear            chlorination            only            clear Which statement is correct? A Only Q and R are suitable for drinking, while P could be used for irrigation., B Only Q and R are suitable for drinking, while P is unsuitable for any purpose., C Only Q is suitable for drinking. R could be used for washing cars and P for irrigation., D P, Q and R are suitable for irrigation and washing cars, but are not suitable for drinking.
questions.text: The table describes three types of water. water type source of water appearance before treatment treatment appearance after treatment P            river            muddy            none            muddy Q river muddy filtration and chlorination clear R            well            clear            chlorination            only            clear Which statement is correct? A Only Q and R are suitable for drinking, while P could be used for irrigation., B Only Q and R are suitable for drinking, while P is unsuitable for any purpose., C Only Q is suitable for drinking. R could be used for washing cars and P for irrigation., D P, Q and R are suitable for irrigation and washing cars, but are not suitable for drinking.
options: A Only Q and R are suitable for drinking, while P could be used for irrigation.
B Only Q and R are suitable for drinking, while P is unsuitable for any purpose.
C Only Q is suitable for drinking. R could be used for washing cars and P for irrigation.
D P, Q and R are suitable for irrigation and washing cars, but are not suitable for drinking.
